# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7.18-slim-jessie

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1 \
    LANG=C.UTF-8

WORKDIR /app

RUN set -eux; \
    apt-get update; \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        gcc \
        make \
        libc6-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
        libpq-dev \
        libxml2-dev \
        libxslt1-dev \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libjpeg-dev \
        libfreetype6-dev \
        curl \
        ca-certificates \
        git \
    ; \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN set -eux; \
    python -m pip install --upgrade "pip<21" "setuptools<45" "wheel<0.35"

COPY requirements*.txt /app/

RUN set -eux; \
    if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi; \
    if [ -f requirements-dev.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements-dev.txt; fi

COPY . /app

EXPOSE 8000

CMD ["python", "app.py"]
